Name: layer_skipping_inference
Title: Dynamic Layer Skipping for Latency-Constrained Inference in Machine Learning Models
Short Hypothesis: Dynamic layer skipping based on real-time Jacobian analysis can significantly enhance inference efficiency under strict latency constraints without compromising accuracy.
Related Work: While previous works have explored training-free frameworks for adaptive inference, none have systematically integrated dynamic layer skipping based on Jacobian analysis to optimize latency in real-time settings. This proposal seeks to fill that gap by providing a comprehensive mechanism for determining which layers to skip during inference, grounded in PAC-Bayes theory.
Abstract: In the quest for efficient machine learning inference under strict latency constraints, we propose a novel approach of dynamic layer skipping based on real-time Jacobian analysis. By evaluating the sensitivity of hidden states across model layers, our framework determines which layers can be skipped without significantly impacting the final prediction accuracy. This training-free method leverages the Jacobian matrix to assess the local behavior of activations, allowing for an adaptive, on-the-fly optimization of inference paths. The proposed mechanism not only respects latency budgets but also maintains a robust throughput-accuracy trade-off. We will empirically validate the approach across various neural architectures and tasks, demonstrating its effectiveness in real-world scenarios where computational resources are limited. Our findings will contribute to the development of more responsive, resource-efficient machine learning models suitable for deployment in latency-critical applications.
Experiments: Conduct a series of experiments on standard benchmark datasets to evaluate the effectiveness of dynamic layer skipping. Measure throughput and accuracy across varying latency constraints.
Implement and compare the proposed layer skipping mechanism against baseline models that do not utilize dynamic skipping. Use metrics such as inference time, model accuracy, and resource utilization.
Perform ablation studies to analyze the impact of different thresholds for Jacobian sensitivity on layer skipping decisions.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include scenarios where skipping layers may lead to significant accuracy degradation, especially in models with intricate dependencies across layers. Additionally, the overhead of real-time Jacobian computation may offset some latency gains in highly resource-constrained environments.
